meta.title: X400 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Jaguar X-Type X400 engine — 2.0D, 2.2D, 2.5 V6, 3.0 V6. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and a clear 60% scrap threshold. Get free quotes from 15 vetted Jaguar specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar X-Type X400 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned X400 engine typically costs £1,500–£3,500 supply only [JAG-QUOTE], depending on engine — the 2.0 diesel sits at £1,500–£2,800, the 2.2 diesel at £1,800–£3,200, and the V6 petrols at £1,500–£3,500. Fitting adds £600–£1,000. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which Jaguar X-Type X400 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 2.0 and 2.2-litre Duratorq diesel units are the most dependable — they're Ford-sourced, robust, and offer strong fuel economy. The V6 petrols have well-documented timing chain issues that can be expensive to fix.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar X-Type X400?

acceptedAnswer.text: Common issues include: timing chain stretch on the 2.5 and 3.0 V6 petrols; injector and turbocharger failure on the 2.0 and 2.2 diesel units; transfer case and AWD system failure on V6 models; and general wear-and-tear on the Ford-sourced suspension components.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar X-Type X400 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on the engine. The diesel units are generally reliable with routine maintenance. The V6 petrols have a known timing chain weakness that can be costly — many X400s are now worth so little that a timing chain repair exceeds the car's value.

mainEntity.name: Which X400 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: We don't recommend outright avoidance, but a 2.5 V6 without documented timing chain history is the one purchase we consistently advise against. The chain stretch risk is well documented, and a replacement can cost £1,500–£3,000 — often more than the car is worth.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my X400 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Key warning signs: V6 — rattle on cold start, timing chain noise, or water pump failure; diesel — rough running, excessive smoke, lack of power, or turbo whine. Use our diagnostic guide to identify the specific symptoms.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Jaguar X-Type X400 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply our 60% Jaguar threshold. A £2,800 diesel recon against a £2,500 X400 is 112% — not worth it. Against a well-kept £4,000 example, the same recon is 70% — borderline. For a V6, a £3,000 recon on a £2,500 car is 120% — a clear financial write-off. Value the car first; if the repair exceeds 60%, walk away.

mainEntity.name: How long do Jaguar X-Type X400 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The diesel units can exceed 200,000 miles with proper servicing — they're Ford-sourced and widely used across the Ford range. The V6 petrols can reach similar mileage, but timing chain issues often appear before 120,000 miles. Chassis and suspension corrosion typically ends the vehicle's life before the engine wears out on many examples.

mainEntity.name: Is the X400 available as both a saloon and an estate?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the Jaguar X-Type was produced in both 4-door saloon and 5-door estate (Sportwagon) body styles. Both share the same X400 chassis code and engine lineup, so they're covered under the same Generation Page. The estate is often favoured for its practicality and distinctive Jaguar styling.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Jaguar X-Type X400 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; block stamping varies by engine. For the diesel, the code is on the block near the injectors. For the V6, it's on the block face below the inlet manifold. Tell us your registration and we'll identify it for free.

hero.h1: Jaguar X-Type X400 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the X400 — from the Ford-sourced 2.0-litre diesel to the 2.5 and 3.0-litre V6 petrols. Real UK replacement costs, honest failure data, and the V6 timing chain and transfer case issues that define this generation's ownership story. Backed by 120+ real UK X400 enquiries in 2025 [JAG-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: Jaguar X-Type X400 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the X400 — from the 2001 launch to the 2009 phase-out, including the 2004 facelift updates.

confidenceScore.title: Confidence Score
confidenceScore.text: All ratings are provisional — no dedicated Engine Page exists for any X400 engine family. The diesel units are Ford-sourced and generally reliable but suffer from injector and turbo issues. The V6 petrols share the Ford Duratec V6's timing chain and water pump problems — the 3.0-litre is considered slightly more robust than the 2.5.

overview.intro: The X400 — better known as the Jaguar X-Type — was Jaguar's compact executive saloon and estate, launched in 2001 and produced until 2009. It was the first Jaguar to be offered with a diesel engine, and it shared its platform and many mechanical components with the Ford Mondeo MK2. The engine lineup comprised Ford-sourced units: the 2.0-litre Duratorq diesel (2.2-litre from 2006) and the 2.5 and 3.0-litre Duratec V6 petrols. A 2004 facelift brought revised styling, but the X400 remained a single generation throughout its production run.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: Ford Mondeo MK2-based (Ford CD132 platform) • Predecessor: none (X-Type was Jaguar's first compact executive) • Successor: XE (2015) • Sibling body styles in this generation: Saloon and Estate (Sportwagon) — both share the same X400 chassis code.
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The X400 generated 120 real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [JAG-VERIFIED], with the 2.0/2.2 diesel variants accounting for the majority of demand.

items.quote: Thin-Data Fallback mode — exactly one paragraph: The 2.0 and 2.2-litre Duratorq diesel units are the most dependable X400 engines — they're Ford-sourced, robust, and offer strong fuel economy. The 3.0-litre V6 petrol is the engine to approach with caution: it's smooth and characterful, but timing chain stretch and water pump failure are well-documented weaknesses that can lead to expensive repairs. For buyers, the diesel is the sensible choice; the V6s are for enthusiasts who want a Jaguar badge with Mondeo running gear and a healthy budget for maintenance.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Best Diesel — Ford-sourced and robust; parts are cheap and widely available.

rows.commonMajorFailure: Timing chain stretch; water pump failure
rows.ownershipVerdict: High Risk — chain work is labour-intensive and can exceed the car's value.

rows.commonMajorFailure: Timing chain stretch; water pump failure
rows.ownershipVerdict: High Risk — same issues as 2.5; more expensive to replace.

economicsRule.title: The X400 rule
economicsRule.text: A £2,800 diesel recon against a £2,500 X400 sits at 112% — brushing our 60% Jaguar threshold. On a sub-£3,000 X400, that recon is simply not viable. The V6s are even worse: a £3,000+ recon against a £2,500 car is a financial write-off. Value the vehicle first, then the repair.

keyTakeaways.question: Which X400 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: 2.0D — cheaper to run, cheaper to fix, and Ford-sourced parts are widely available.

keyTakeaways.question: Which X400 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: 3.0 V6 — higher fuel consumption, higher replacement cost, same timing chain risks.

keyTakeaways.question: Which X400 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: 2.0D with documented service history — the sensible buy in a low-value market.

problems.title: V6 Timing Chain Stretch
problems.rootCause: The Ford Duratec V6 suffers from timing chain stretch and tensioner wear, which can cause rattling on cold start and, in severe cases, valve timing drift. If the chain snaps, piston-to-valve contact is catastrophic. The 2.5-litre is the more problematic of the two.

tieredCosts.work: Timing chain, tensioner and guide replacement

problems.recommendation: If your V6 X400 develops a rattle on cold start, have the timing chain inspected immediately — but be realistic: a £600–£1,500 repair often exceeds the car's market value. On a low-value X400, it may be time to walk away.

cta.label: Compare V6 engine prices for your X400 →

problems.title: 2.0/2.2 Diesel Injector Failure
problems.rootCause: The Ford Duratorq diesel's injectors can fail due to electrical faults or internal wear, leading to rough running, excessive smoke, and poor fuel economy. Injector leaks can also cause fuel dilution of the engine oil.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of rough running or excessive smoke on a diesel X400, have the injectors tested — a £300–£800 injector replacement is far cheaper than the £2,000–£3,500 engine replacement that follows if a failed injector causes piston damage.

cta.label: Compare diesel engine prices for your X400 →

problems.title: Transfer Case and AWD System Failure
problems.affectedModels: All X400 AWD variants (V6 models only — diesels were front-wheel drive).
problems.rootCause: The X400's viscous coupling transfer case can fail, leading to driveline vibration, binding, and eventual loss of all-wheel-drive function. This is a common non-engine failure that can be expensive to fix.

tieredCosts.work: Transfer case fluid change and inspection

problems.recommendation: Have the transfer case fluid changed every 60,000 miles — a £400–£900 fluid service is far cheaper than the £1,500–£2,800 replacement that follows a failed transfer case.

cta.label: Compare X400 transfer case repair quotes →

problems.title: 2.0/2.2 Diesel Turbocharger Failure
problems.rootCause: The Ford Duratorq diesel's turbocharger can fail due to oil starvation, worn bearings, or variable geometry sticking. Common symptoms include lack of power, excessive smoke, and turbo whine.
problems.recommendation: If your diesel X400 develops a lack of power or turbo whine, have the turbo inspected immediately — a £400–£900 turbo repair is far cheaper than the £2,000–£3,500 engine replacement that follows if turbo debris enters the engine.

cta.label: Compare diesel turbo repair quotes for your X400 →

replacementCosts.h2: X400 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition
replacementCosts.note: No Generation variance note required — no Engine Page baseline exists for these engines yet. Costs are sourced from UK specialist suppliers [JAG-QUOTE].
